behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Backoff correctness depends on retry-attempt state carried across failures plus timer scheduling; a pure function test can miss state-reset or cap-enforcement bugs in the real retry loop.
behaviors.test_description: Wire retry manager + backoff policy + fake clock + stub transport that returns repeated 429s. Assert observed scheduled delays are 1x, 2x, 4x... capped at max, across actual retry attempts.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is response parsing/classification wired to retry policy. Mocking only the classifier or only the backoff function can hide mismatches in status/header interpretation.
behaviors.test_description: Use a stub HTTP layer to return realistic rate-limit responses (e.g., 429 and optional retry headers). Verify retry path is triggered and computed delay matches policy/header precedence.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: This requires validating end-to-end retry orchestration (request preservation, delayed resend, and promise resolution), not just single-function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Stub transport to fail with rate-limit once, then succeed. Advance fake clock past wait; assert second call payload matches original request and caller receives success without manual retry handling.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Typed error emission depends on retry-loop terminal path and error mapping at exhaustion; unit tests of error class alone miss incorrect throw path/type conversion.
behaviors.test_description: Configure small max retries; stub transport to always rate-limit. Run full call path and assert rejection is instance of `QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata, not a generic/transport error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Real risk is control-flow bugs across modules (attempt counter, quota accounting, scheduler) that can create non-terminating loops; mocking internals can hide this.
behaviors.test_description: With transport always returning rate-limit, execute `call()` under fake clock and hard test timeout. Assert exact max-attempt count, final terminal error, and no further scheduled retries after completion.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Shared-quota correctness is about coordination between concurrent callers and shared state; single-function tests cannot expose cross-call race/order interactions.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate one client/account bucket. Fire many concurrent `call()` invocations against a stub API with known quota behavior. Verify aggregate attempts respect one shared token budget, not per-caller independent budgets.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: Ordering guarantees emerge from queue + scheduler + async wake-up behavior. Unit tests of queue alone can miss reorder bugs in dispatch integration.
behaviors.test_description: Submit N requests quickly while dispatch is blocked/rate-limited, then release capacity stepwise. Capture actual send sequence in stub transport and assert FIFO by original submission order.
